title: #all-hands: 3076543210
preview:
Diego Park: Quick heads-up — all-hands today at 11:00am PT. Agenda: 1) Exec state & metrics; 2) Dedicated capacity expansion launch callouts; 3) New usage quota policy and migration path; 4) Observability + cost tooling demo; 5) Q&A. Recording and slides will be posted after. Drop short questions here or submit via the form: https://forms.redwood-inference/c/allhands-ask

Ava Chen: excited — will share a 2-slide summary of strategic priorities and hiring asks.

Maya Singh: quick Q — for the quota policy, is this going to be opt-in or enforced automatically for existing hosted customers?

Diego Park: good q. tl;dr: opt-in for tiered billing customers for the first 30 days, then default to soft-enforcement with a 30-day notice. We're piloting with three enterprise customers first. Full migration plan in the follow-up doc.

Jon Lee: will this change any of the Dedicated SLA or autoscale behavior? ops needs to know if we should update runbooks.

Diego Park: Dedicated unaffected. This is only hosted API quota controls + suggested defaults. But we will add an ops flag for early rollouts. Runbooks should get a note — tagging eng-sre and eng-platform to review.

Ivy (Legal): flagged — need to confirm the notice language for regulated customers before we auto-enforce. Can the legal team review the customer emails before send?

Diego Park: absolutely. Legal review before any enforcement emails. We'll schedule a 30-min sync after the all-hands.

Diego Park: Thanks everyone who joined. Recording: https://redwood-inference.company/recordings/allhands-3076543210 (auto-uploaded). Slides: https://redwood-inference.company/slides/allhands-3076543210.pdf

Ava Chen: Key takeaways: - Maintain focus on latency & cost for next two quarters - Prioritize Private deployment sales play for regulated verticals - Add 4 headcount for infra + 2 for solutions engineering (attached in hiring doc)

Maya Singh: follow-up on quotas: we're publishing a migration playbook by EOD Friday — includes customer messaging, API flags, and opt-in flow.

Jon Lee: ops follow-ups: 1) update runbooks for early rollout flag by 2025-04-08; 2) add alert for quota-throttling spikes; 3) coordinate canary with eng-sre.

Ivy (Legal): I will review the messaging draft by Thursday and flag any regulatory wording.

Diego Park: ACTIONS (short):
- Diego to publish migration playbook (EOD Fri)
- Ava to send exec summary + hiring ask to board (Mon)
- Maya to share migration API spec + rollout timeline (Fri)
- Jon to update runbooks and create alert (2025-04-08)
- Ivy to sign off on customer notice (Thu)

Diego Park: If you raised a question during the meeting and want a dedicated follow-up, reply here with "follow-up: <topic>" and I'll assign an owner. Thanks! :raised_hands:

Maya Singh: follow-up: quota edge-cases (burst customers).

Jon Lee: follow-up: canary schedule for eng-sre (slot request sent).

Sam Nguyen: Action items summarized: playbook PR (Marta), dashboard (Kai), QA prompt set and smoke eval (Priya). This thread is source of truth until PR lands. Thanks all. :thumbsup:

title: #all-hands: 2345678901
preview:
Sam Nguyen: Thanks for joining the follow-up thread from today's all-hands. I want a concise record of the runtime/canary decisions we made.
Ava Chen: I'll paste the leadership answers so folks can act. Short and actionable.
Priya Rao: first question: what's our formal definition of 'regression' for kernel canaries?
Ava Chen: regression = any of: (1) canary p99 latency > baseline p99 * 1.12 for 10m, (2) error rate > 0.4% sustained for 10m, (3) eval prompt F1 drop >= 2% on core prompt set. Any hit triggers auto-rollback and an alert.
Kai Patel: are those baselines per-model or global?
Ava Chen: per-model baseline. Baselines are the last stable release window median; stored in telemetry. ENG-5040 will expose an API to query baselines.
Marta Lopez: follow-up about targets: what are our service targets we should show in docs and to customers?
Sam Nguyen: agreed we need public-facing SLOs. For Dedicated: short-turn p95 <= 120ms, p99 <= 300ms. Long-context (8k+) p95 <= 600ms, p99 <= 1.25s. For Hosted free-tier we'll say "best-effort" with typical medians in benchmarks.
Diego Rivera: for batching: any short guidance for devs that onboard models? We saw head-of-line blocking in the last rollout.
Ava Chen: recommended defaults: 1) route short queries (<256 tokens) to short route with microbatching target 64ms latency cap, 2) long queries to separate pool with batching optimized for throughput. If mixed traffic is unavoidable, enable sequence-length aware batching or set dynamic max-batch-size to avoid H-O-L.
Priya Rao: can we get a sample canary command in the playbook? Micro-canary recipe would help SEs.
Ava Chen: adding example now below playbook and in this thread:
```
# micro-canary: 10-minute run at 20% of target QPS
canary-run --model=kernel-v3-opt --qps=50 --duration=600 --ratio=0.2 --tags=team-canary
# post-run: check dashboards/canaries/<run-id> and run evals
```
Kai Patel: dashboard pieces: I'll put p50/p95/p99, error rate, and request size histogram. Also adding an 'anomaly' band for canary baseline comparison.
Marta Lopez: I'll own the playbook PR. Plan: PR by Thu EOD with sections: thresholds, rollback, play-by-play canary steps, postmortem checklist.
Sam Nguyen: ownership noted. What about communication to customers when kernels change that could affect outputs?
Ava Chen: release policy: For behavioral/determinism-impacting kernel changes, we post release notes + alert banner for Dedicated customers 72 hours before rollout. For Private deployments, contacts will be notified and given migration notes.
Diego Rivera: question on measurements: are the latency numbers server-only? or include gateway/proxy?
Ava Chen: the SLOs we publish are server-only for consistency across infra; but Console will show end-to-end numbers too. Note ENG-5062 for end-to-end telemetry rollout.
Priya Rao: I'll assemble the core eval prompt set and share by Mon. Also propose adding a 'smoke' eval that runs pre/post canary automatically.
Marta Lopez: good idea. Add smoke eval to the canary pipeline.
Kai Patel: about auto-rollback: who gets paged? Ops on-call + kernel owners. I'll add runbook steps to the alert.
Sam Nguyen: ops-bot: please create tickets for the three follow-ups we flagged.
ops-bot: Note: ENG-5040, ENG-5062, ENG-5075 created and linked to runtime/roadmap epic.
Ava Chen: small note for engineers: if you see p99 spikes after a kernel tweak, check sequence length mix and tail queueing; often it's a microbatching threshold interaction. Also please attach perf traces to the canary ticket (nvprof / nsight sampling).
Diego Rivera: I'll share a short troubleshooting checklist in the playbook PR (nvprof, mem alloc traces, attention kernel stalls).
Sam Nguyen: perfect. Let's treat this thread as the single source until the PR lands. Thanks all for quick turn. :thumbsup:

title: #all-hands: 2325412345
preview:
Sam Nguyen: Thanks everyone for the all-hands — quick follow-up thread for the performance playbook items we promised to clarify.
Ava Chen: putting the leaders' answers here so folks can action.
Priya Rao: can we get the concrete p95/p99 targets for the two critical paths (short chat vs long context spanning 8k+ tokens)?
Ava Chen: short chat SLO we discussed = p95 < 120ms, p99 < 300ms. Long-context targets are looser: p95 < 600ms, p99 < 1.2s. These are service-level targets for Dedicated tier.
Kai Patel: are those measured server-side (inference-only) or end-to-end (proxy + network)?
Ava Chen: server-side inference only. Console dashboards will show end-to-end soon — tracking work item ENG-4892.
Marta Lopez: follow-up: what's the canary threshold for kernel changes? I want to know when we auto-rollback.
Ava Chen: canary rules: if canary p99 latency increases by >12% vs baseline OR error-rate >0.5% for 10 minutes -> auto-roll back. We also flag quality regressions with eval sets.
Leo Zhang: for batching changes, any guidance on per-model batch size defaults vs workload?
Ava Chen: default batch size is 32 for Llama-like small models, 8 for 70B-ish; but batching policy should be workload-aware. Playbook excerpt: try 2x current median batch first, measure CPU/GPU mem pressure + tail latencies.
Priya Rao: can we include a quick snippet in the playbook for how to run a micro-canary?
Ava Chen: yes — quick example added below for now:
```
# run a 5-min canary against pool A
runner canary --model=my-kernel-v3 --qps=80 --duration=300 --tags=canary-a
# check telemetry: dashboards/inference/canaries
```
Sam Nguyen: who owns the docs update and the console dashboard widget?
Marta Lopez: I'll own the playbook doc update (PR by EOD Fri).
Kai Patel: I'll take the dashboard widget; will add the p95/p99 series + request distribution heatmap.
Ava Chen: action items: 1) Marta update playbook with canary config + rollback rules, 2) Kai add dashboard and alert, 3) Priya assemble the QA prompt set for kernel regression checks.
Priya Rao: assigning QA prompt set now. Expect initial set by Tue.
Leo Zhang: small ask — add a short section on "sequence-length aware batching" (we saw regressions when short+long mix created head-of-line blocking).
Ava Chen: good call. Playbook will recommend separating short/long routes or enabling dynamic batching thresholds.
ops-bot: Note: ENG-4892, ENG-4901, ENG-4902 created and linked to the roadmap epic.
Sam Nguyen: last: how do we communicate to customers when we change kernel behavior that might affect token-level determinism?
Ava Chen: we should publish required breaking-change notes in the release notes + console banner for Dedicated customers 72h prior. For private/on-prem, engage the account owners directly.
Marta Lopez: I'll draft the release note template and post in #product when ready.
Kai Patel: ping me when dashboards are in staging, I'll sanity-check alerts.
Sam Nguyen: thanks — this thread will be the source of truth until the playbook PR lands. :thumbsup:
Ava Chen: adding link to PR here once open.
Priya Rao: putting test harness run schedule into calendar — will coordinate with infra to reserve capacity for canaries.
Leo Zhang: quick troubleshooting tip for folks: if you see increased p99 after a kernel tweak, run `nvprof` on the canary node and compare samples — often reveals attention kernel stalls.
Sam Nguyen: perfect. Closing out once PR & dashboard done. Appreciate the quick turn. :rocket:

title: #all-hands: 2156789012
preview:

title: #all-hands: 1917777777
preview:
Priya Desai: Thanks everyone for coming to today's all-hands. Posting a live thread for follow-ups on GTM priorities, pipeline health, and partner asks. Drop questions and we'll answer during the next 24h.

Ethan Park: Quick ask — can we clarify the new partner-led deal routing? who owns initial discovery vs. Redwood AE?

Priya Desai: Good question. Short answer: partner-sourced leads => partner engages until SQL, then handoff to Redwood AE for commercial. Partner success stays engaged post-close. Will share playbook link.

Aisha Khan: @Priya can you confirm crediting/commission for partner-influenced renewals? sales ops needs the rule for quotas.

Priya Desai: Yes — renewals credited to the account owner unless contract explicitly routed to partner. Sales Ops to formalize by EOW.

Diego Morales: Anything changing for Dedicated vs Hosted pricing in pipeline motions? Some prospects ask for reserved capacity discounts.

Priya Desai: No instant changes. We have a guided discount matrix (see /docs/gtm/discount-matrix). For large Dedicated commits we can escalate to deals desk for bespoke pricing.

Jordan Lee: On the product side — are there roadmap items that should be called out in sales decks for Q2? customers ask about private deployment timelines.

Priya Desai: Yes — highlight Private GA milestones: VPC automation (June), KMS integration (July). Call out Dedicated autoscaling improvements too. I'll send a short bullets slide.

Ethan Park: Follow-up: do we have a clear SLA promise to give when prospects ask about multi-region failover?

Priya Desai: Default SLA for Hosted is 99.9. For Dedicated we can offer 99.95 with proper capacity commitments. Multi-region failover is an add-on & requires solutions engagement.

Aisha Khan: Action item: Sales Ops to update quote templates with the SLA language. @Ethan please coordinate. :+1:

Ethan Park: noted. will sync with Sales Ops tomorrow.

sam-deploy-bot: Reminder — GTM playbook doc updated: https://redwood.internal/gtm/playbook (edited)

Diego Morales: Partners — are we approving new ISV partners faster now? we've had a couple waiting on security review.

Priya Desai: Security review backlog reduced. @Aisha please triage outstanding partner security requests this week and flag any blockers.

Aisha Khan: On it. I have two in the queue; will surface any special requirements.

Jordan Lee: Can we get a one-pager for engineers on partner integrations expectations (auth patterns, data flows) — that will help solution architects during POCs.

Diego Morales: +1. We keep re-explaining the same details during demos.

Priya Desai: Good idea. @Jordan can draft with Eng-Platform and Product. we'll publish to the partner portal.

Jordan Lee: Will do. aiming for a draft by Friday.

Ethan Park: Metrics check — what's the target pipeline coverage for next quarter?

Priya Desai: Target is 4x ARR coverage entering Q3, with 30% of opens from partner-sourced channels. We should also track velocity — average sales cycle target is 60 days for SMB, 120 for enterprise.

Aisha Khan: Any change in quota allocations for AE team given Q2 focus on Dedicated and Private?

Priya Desai: Minor re-weight toward Dedicated quota for a subset of AEs who are specialization-trained. We'll announce role-specific quotas in the next sales sync.

Diego Morales: For marketing: should we amplify partner-led case studies? got a request from content team.

Priya Desai: Yes — prioritize 2 partner co-sell stories this quarter. Content to coordinate with the referenced partners for joint assets.

Ethan Park: One operational question — if a partner signs up for sandbox Dedicated capacity for evaluation, what is the standard PVL (provisioning lead time)?

Priya Desai: Typically 3-5 business days. If infra is constrained it may take longer; Eng-Infra will notify.

sam-deploy-bot: Deploy note: upcoming Dedicated autoscaler rollout scheduled for 2026-06-10 — might impact provisioning windows.

Jordan Lee: Noted. We'll communicate that to prospects.

Aisha Khan: Action summary: 1) Sales Ops update quote SLA language (Ethan) 2) Partner security triage (Aisha) 3) Partner-integration one-pager (Jordan + Eng) 4) Publish Dedicated/Private highlights slide (Priya) 5) Content to prioritize 2 co-sell case studies (Marketing).

Priya Desai: Thanks — I'll follow up with calendar invites for owners. If anyone else has a question drop it here and we'll answer.

Ethan Park: thanks Priya. adding a note to pipeline board. :rocket:

Diego Morales: thanks all — appreciate the clarity.

Jordan Lee: thanks — will post draft one-pager here when ready.

sam-deploy-bot: Thread archived in 24h if no new messages.

title: #all-hands: 1842509999
preview:
Noah Kim: Quick Q from the all-hands: can we get a concise definition of patch vs minor vs major for the release calendar?
Zara Hughes: Yup. Short answer: patch = urgent bug/security fix; minor = regular feature/iterative work; major = API or infra contract change or anything that needs cross-team migration.
Maya Singh: Also need clarity on frequency: you mentioned 'cadence' in the talk — is that frozen or guidance?
Ava Chen (CTO): Guidance. Plan is: weekly small patches as needed; a two-week minor cadence (feature train); and a monthly window for bigger platform/major-compat releases. Exceptions allowed with RFC and coordinating owners.
Ben Carter (EngOps): For rollouts we'll use staged expansion with monitoring gates. Suggested default: 1% -> 10% -> 50% -> 100%. Smaller services can skip steps.
Noah Kim: What monitoring gates should be in the PR?
Zara Hughes: Include: baseline error rate, baseline p95 latency, a list of synthetic "golden" prompts for perf and correctness checks, and any route-specific counters. Ben will add a small checklist to the PR template. :white_check_mark:
Oncall-bot: 
```
Default rollout timeline:
- 1% (5m checks)
- 10% (15m checks)
- 50% (30m checks)
- 100% (1h stabilization)
Auto-cut triggers (any one):
- 5xx rate increase > 0.2% absolute for 10m
- request error rate > 3x baseline for 5m
- p95 latency > 1.5x baseline for 15m
- increase in model hallucination signals (eval drift) > threshold
```
Maya Singh: I like the 1% start for risky changes. How involved is oncall at each step?
Ben Carter (EngOps): Oncall watches for alerts and has authority to pause/rollback. They do not need to manually ack each expansion unless an alert fires. Owners must post progress in #release-updates.
Noah Kim: What are the quality gates pre-merge for a minor?
Zara Hughes: Required: passing unit tests, staging smoke, perf regression test against the golden prompt set (no >4% regression in throughput or p95), and model-output regression check for any generation/model-selection changes. E2E tests when routing/model selector is affected.
Ava Chen (CTO): Important: any change affecting model selection, quantization, or tokenization needs a model-eval run and a second reviewer from ML infra.
Maya Singh: Post-release: when do we expect incident writeups?
Zara Hughes: Any customer-impacting regression or rollback => 72h mini postmortem filled in the playbook. Less severe issues should still get a short incident note within a week.
Noah Kim: SLA tie-ins: does the cadence change when error budget is low?
Ava Chen (CTO): Yes. If monthly error budget remaining < 15% we auto-enforce a freeze for non-critical changes until engineering triage clears the budget. EngOps publishes alerts at 50% and again at 15%.
Ben Carter (EngOps): Enforcement: automatic block in release tool when <15% error budget. Exceptions need exec signoff documented in the release ticket.
Maya Singh: Branching policy for hotfixes?
Zara Hughes: Hotfixes: branch from stable (patch/*), include changelog and tests, and get two approvers if touching public APIs. Merge back to main after validation.
Noah Kim: Can Ben drop the PR checklist and a short cheatsheet into #eng-process?
Ben Carter (EngOps): Will do. I'll post a one-pager with the default rollout snippet and PR checklist by EOD tomorrow.
Ava Chen (CTO): Thanks — we'll add the one-pager to the onboarding playbook and link it from the release RFC template.
Noah Kim: Appreciated, this clears up a lot.

title: #all-hands: 1839004567
preview:
Samir Patel: Quick follow-up from today's all-hands — can leadership clarify the new release cadence and what counts as a patch vs minor vs major?
Priya Nair: Great q. TL;DR: patches = hotfix/SEV1 regressions, minor = regular feature releases, major = breaking changes or platform upgrades that need coord with infra.
Lena Ortiz: Also want to know where the rollback/autocut threshold is — we had a canary that spiked p95 last week.
Ava Chen (CTO): High level: aiming for a biweekly feature train (every 2 weeks) for non-blocking improvements, monthly non-breaking platform release window for larger work, and immediate patches for incidents.
Ava Chen (CTO): Patch criteria: any customer-facing correctness or reliability regression (SEV1/SEV2) or security fix -> ship as patch ASAP. Minor = backward-compatible features that pass QA. Major = infra or API contract changes, requires RFC + cross-team signoff.
Diego Ruiz: Can you spell out the canary rollout steps and thresholds?
Priya Nair: Canary plan: 5% -> 25% -> 100% over controlled windows. Metrics to watch: error rate, latency p95, and request drop rate. Auto-rollback triggers if error rate > 3x baseline AND sustained for 5m, OR p95 latency > 2x baseline for 10m.
Riley (Eng Manager): Are those thresholds per-route or global?
Priya Nair: Per-route where possible; if metric collection missing, fallback to service-level. We should add missing route-level meters as tech debt. :memo:
Samir Patel: What's the role of oncall in a rollout? Do they need to ack every canary step?
Morgan Lee: Oncall is primary for rapid rollback decisions. They don't need to ack 5% -> 25% unless they see alarms, but they should be watching the rollout channel and confirm within 10m if an alert fires.
Ava Chen (CTO): We want to avoid micromanaging every step. Oncall has authority to pause/rollback. Release owner is responsible for monitoring and for having a rollback plan in the PR description.
Lena Ortiz: Quality bar check — what minimal tests are required before a minor/feature train lands?
Priya Nair: Required: unit tests, smoke integration test, and a perf regression check against a reference prompt set with a threshold (no >5% regression in p95 token latency). E2E tests if the change touches routing or model selector.
Samir Patel: And post-release — do we require a postmortem for patch/rollback events?
Riley (Eng Manager): Postmortem required for any rollback or customer-impacting incident. Short blameless postmortem template in Confluence should be filled within 72h.
Diego Ruiz: SLA/SLO question — are there updated SLO targets tied to these cadences?
Ava Chen (CTO): SLOs remain: 99.9% availability for hosted API, latency SLOs per model/route documented in Console. Error budget policy: if weekly error budget drops below 20% remaining, we restrict non-essential releases and prioritize stabilization.
Morgan Lee: Who enforces release freezes when error budget is low?
Priya Nair: EngOps will publish a warning when error budget < 50% and apply a hard freeze if < 20% (exceptions require exec signoff).
Samir Patel: Can someone paste the rollout example we should copy into PR templates?
deploy-bot: 
```
Rollout plan:
- Canary 5% (10m monitoring)
- Expand to 25% (20m monitoring)
- Full rollout 100% (30m monitoring)
Rollback criteria:
- error_rate > 3x baseline for 5m
- p95 latency > 2x baseline for 10m
- increase in 5xxs > 0.5% absolute
```
Priya Nair: That snippet is the baseline. Add any service-specific checks under "extra checks" in the PR template.
Lena Ortiz: One last thing — does this change how we do hotfix branch naming / cherry-pick policy?
Riley (Eng Manager): Hotfixes should be branched from stable, tagged as patch/* and include changelog note. Cherry-picks to main happen after validation; small teams may do direct cherry-pick with two approvers.
Samir Patel: Thanks all — this helps. Would be great if EngOps could post a short doc with timelines and checklist.
Priya Nair: On it. I'll draft a short checklist and drop it in #eng-process by EOD tomorrow. :thumbsup:
Ava Chen (CTO): Thanks — we'll align this into the playbook and add to next week's onboarding.
Morgan Lee: :eyes: will review checklist once posted.
Samir Patel: Appreciate it.

title: #all-hands: 1768501234
preview:
Ava Chen: thanks everyone for joining the all-hands — quick follow-up on the roadmap items we touched on. 2 quick asks: can product/eng clarify any hard deps between API GA, Console redesign, and Optimize features?
Maya Patel: short answer — there are a few hard deps but several things can be parallelized. i can list them.
Liam O'Neill: +1 — from eng side we need the new routing infra ready before we push the Console's model-selection UI (otherwise it's just cosmetic).
Sofia Gutierrez: to be explicit: Console redesign MVP depends on the routing infra for model pinning and canary flags. not the entire Dedicated stack.
Raj Singh: routing infra = smart-router + model-variant registry + rollout service. the registry work is biggest blocker (3 sprints).
Ella Park: for UX planning we need a stable API surface for model metadata — ideally a small read-only endpoint. that can land earlier.
Maya Patel: propose breakdown:
- API: GA for basic generation + embeddings (core SDK) — target: end of Q1. depends on swagger + auth work only.
- Console MVP: model-explore + rollout controls — target: mid-Q2. depends on routing infra registry (blocking) and on an approved metadata contract from API.
- Optimize: batching/quantization recommender (beta) — target: late Q2. depends on telemetry pipeline (ingest + eval sets) and the Console's eval viewer (for UX).
Ava Chen: do we have contingency if registry slips?
Raj Singh: fallback is to ship Console with read-only selection and warn that pin/rollout controls are disabled. not ideal but keeps momentum.
Liam O'Neill: engineering note: if registry slips >2 sprints we should ship Console without live rollouts and enable via feature flag when registry is ready.
Sofia Gutierrez: action item for product/design: prepare a fallback mock so we can user-test Console flows without full rollout backend.
Ella Park: will produce mock screens by next Wednesday.
Maya Patel: risks: Optimize depends on reliable eval telemetry. infra is ~1 sprint behind due to elastic ingest work. if that slips, Optimize beta will shift.
Ava Chen: can we mitigate by using a trimmed eval pipeline for the beta?
Raj Singh: yes — we can stand up a lightweight ingest (no retention guarantees) to unblock Optimize beta, but it costs ~0.5 engineer for 3 weeks.
Liam O'Neill: OK to reprioritize a half-sprint of infra to unblock Optimize if product signs off.
Sofia Gutierrez: product signs off on that trade for beta. pls confirm costs in the weekly planning sync.
Maya Patel: summarizing next steps:
1) Raj to give exact ETA on registry by Monday (1768580000 window).
2) Ella to deliver fallback mock screens by Wed.
3) Raj to scope lightweight eval ingest and provide estimate.
4) Liam to prepare Console feature-flag plan to ship without live rollouts if needed.
5) Maya to update the public roadmap page and signal any shifts to sales.
Ava Chen: thank you — leaders, please post status in this thread after your Monday planning.
all-hands-bot: meeting notes saved to the company drive: https://docs.internal/redwood/all-hands/1768501234-notes
Maya Patel: noted. i'll update the roadmap PR and ping sales + success channels. :thumbsup:
Liam O'Neill: bump if i don't post engineering plan by EOD Monday.
Raj Singh: will post ETA + scope then.
Sofia Gutierrez: thanks everyone — this helps prioritize user testing and release comms.
Ava Chen: <3
